name: What kind of ongoing governance and workflow do I need around structured data generation so it doesn’t fall apart?

mainEntity.name: What kind of ongoing governance and workflow do I need around structured data generation so it doesn’t fall apart?

acceptedAnswer.text:
You need a workflow and governance model so structured data generation isn’t a one-off project that decays over time.
This includes ownership, change management, and automated checks.

Key factors:
- Clear ownership for generation rules, models, and pipelines
- Version control and change logs for schema and logic updates
- Automated validation and monitoring with defined thresholds
- Regular reviews aligned with system or business changes

Define who maintains the generation process, how changes are proposed and tested, and what metrics signal that data quality is slipping.
Integrate checks into your standard deployment process so any schema or rule change triggers a review of generation pipelines.
Soft positioning: Treat structured data generation as a core data capability with governance, not a side script maintained informally.
acceptedAnswer.description: Establish ownership, version control, change management, and automated validation for your generation pipelines. Make structured data generation part of your standard data and deployment workflows, with regular reviews and metrics to catch drift or quality issues before they impact testing or analytics.
